assertion_results.text: Agent checks current auth status with gcloud auth list
assertion_results.evidence: Line 28 (Step 1b): 'gcloud auth list' — run as the first auth check with expected output examples for both authenticated and unauthenticated states (lines 36-50).

assertion_results.text: Agent recommends gcloud auth login for local interactive development
assertion_results.evidence: Line 81 (Step 2a): 'gcloud auth login' — explicitly recommended as 'Authenticate gcloud CLI (interactive browser login)' with full description of browser-based flow (lines 84-89).

assertion_results.text: Agent mentions gcloud auth application-default login for application default credentials
assertion_results.evidence: Line 101 (Step 2b): 'gcloud auth application-default login' — presented as a separate step titled 'Set up Application Default Credentials (ADC)' with explanation of the credential file location (line 107).

assertion_results.text: Agent explains what ADC is and why it's needed in addition to regular auth
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 111-113: '`gcloud auth login` authenticates the `gcloud` CLI tool itself. `gcloud auth application-default login` sets up credentials for your application code that uses Google client libraries. They are separate credential stores.' Also lines 286-288: 'Two types of auth -- gcloud auth login is for the CLI. gcloud auth application-default login is for your application code. You need both for local dev.'

assertion_results.text: Agent shows gcloud projects list to discover available projects
assertion_results.evidence: Line 152 (Step 4): 'gcloud projects list --format="table(projectId,name,projectNumber)"' — presented with expected output example (lines 163-168).

assertion_results.text: Agent recommends setting a default project with gcloud config set project
assertion_results.evidence: Line 181 (Step 5): 'gcloud config set project YOUR_PROJECT_ID' — recommended with confirmation flow ('Shall I proceed?') and verification step (line 190: 'gcloud config get-value project').

assertion_results.text: Output distinguishes between user auth (gcloud auth login) and application auth (gcloud auth application-default login)
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 111-113 explicitly distinguish: 'gcloud auth login authenticates the gcloud CLI tool itself. gcloud auth application-default login sets up credentials for your application code that uses Google client libraries. They are separate credential stores.' Lines 286-288 reiterate: 'gcloud auth login is for the CLI. gcloud auth application-default login is for your application code.'

assertion_results.text: Agent does NOT recommend service account keys for local development
assertion_results.evidence: No mention of service account keys, JSON key files, or GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS pointing to a key file anywhere in the output. The recommended approach is exclusively interactive user auth via gcloud auth login and gcloud auth application-default login.
